titleStmt.title: Letter from Charles Hezlet to his mother, 26 September 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: Charles Owen Hezlet

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: Letter from Lt. Col. Charles Owen Hezlet (1892-1965) to his mother, Emily Mary Linzee (née Owen). At the time this letter was written Hezlet was serving in the British army in France. In the letter Hezlet writes that he is having an exciting time as they make their 'big advance'. He goes on to mention a shell attack which destroyed all their mess and gramophone records. However, Hezlet does note that some compensation will be provided.Charles Hezlet was a major in the army and an amateur international golfer. He played for the Royal Portrush Golf Club.

msIdentifier.repository: Public Record Office of Northern Ireland

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
France 26th Sept My dearest Mammy Thanks for
your letter. We have had a
very exciting time lately, we
have made a big advance
yesterday and to-day, I expect
you now all about it in
the papers, also we have
had rather a catastrophe
a Hun 5.9 shell pitch right
in the middle of our Mess
luckily for us nobody was
in it at the time and
nobody hurt at all, it
was very lucky, unfortunately
everything was destroyed all
our mess kit and gramophone
and records, my field glasses
were smashed into little
bits also a coat and
my armchair and lots of
other things, I hope we
we get some compensation
for it, as we lost about
50Â£ worth of stuff between
the lot of it us, I am
still commanding 33rdLeags. Love to all
your aff son Charles Hezlet
Back of envelope, no text to transcribe
Back of envelope, no text to transcribe
